Patch Set 2:

(6 comments)

Patch Set 2:

I think this is looking very promising, but I think it makes sense to merge this with the previous patch, unless we are sure the smaller patch will be useful by itself?

I'm also a bit worried that we are not submitting the IO as efficiently as it could.  For 512k groups with 512k bitmaps (640TiB), we would generate 20000 reads/seeks if reading with flex_bg size of 256 (default for Lustre OSTs).  This is "only" about 200s @ 100 seek/sec to read all of the bitmaps.  If we need to seek for every bitmap this would take 14h, so we need to be sure that the IOs are merged.

Patch Set 2:

(3 comments)

Patch Set 2:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 2:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 2:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 2:

# cat /sys/block/vdb/queue/scheduler 
[none] 

AFAIU, virtual block devices in 4+ kernel don't use old IO framework with IO schedulers, instead mq infrastructure is used which is no-op.

Patch Set 2:

The kernel is always free to discard readahead requests, so probably we shouldn't use RAHEAD for at least the next flex_bg worth of bitmaps that we actually care about.  Also, if the group is marked UNINIT then no read is done for the bitmap since it is empty. 

Also, you could watch iostat to see what the disk IO size is?

Patch Set 3:

unfortunately I can't check disk iostats in kvm - basically because virtual blockdev uses no-op scheduler. I tried to use virts-scsi, but still it uses MQ..
the tiny testing box I use locally has PSU which is not enough for legacy rotational drive - getting IO errors :(

Alex, could you please refresh this patch based on the previous version. 

For testing, what do you think about creating sparse loopback devices, then MDRAID-0 them together to make a large OST volume, format it (with the default lazy_itable_init and lazy_journal_init), then set the bitmaps to 90% full with your script and see how long it takes to mount without and with your patch?  That shouldn't take huge amounts of space (5M block bitmaps is 20GB), which should be most of the space usage for A 600TB filesystem.

Patch Set 14: Code-Review+1

> I don't see significant improvement with dm-slow using this patch.
 > probably that's because IO scheduler don't merge requests properly.
 > IO stack is crazy: dm-slow -> loop -> xfs -> tmpfs.
 > it makes sense to give it a try on a real disk.

Hmm, I'm confused about this comment.  In LU-12988 you write:

 # echo "0 134217728000  delay /dev/loop0 0 5" |
   dmsetup create dm-slow
 # # unpatched ldiskfs # #
 # time mount -t lustre /dev/mapper/dm-slow /mnt/ost2
 ^C
 real	27m42.190s
 user	0m0.000s
 sys	0m0.002s
 # # according to vmstat mount was going to take ~11 hours # #

 # # patched ldiskfs, cache wiped # #
 # echo 3 >/proc/sys/vm/drop_caches
 # time mount -t lustre /dev/mapper/dm-slow /mnt/ost2
 
 real	0m20.364s
 user	0m0.192s
 sys	0m1.103s

So I'd think that adding something like this into the commit message would be enough, as it clearly shows an improvement?  It still isn't really clear to me that keeping the "skip non-loaded groups at cr=0/1" patch separate makes sense, since that won't provide any improvement at mount time without this patch to start prefetching the groups.

Patch Set 14:

(1 comment)

I think if you are relatively satisfied with this patch, we could land it to master, and cherry-pick it to b2_12, which would make it a lot easier to test on some large system.

Patch Set 14:

> Hmm, I'm confused about this comment.  In LU-12988 you write:
this is specifically about prefetching, I couldn't demonstrate any visible improvement, but that's to be verified on a real hardware, IMO.
as discussed above all demonstrated (with dm-slow) improvement of mount time is due to another patch to skip uninitialized groups.
I'll add the results to the commit message and have a look at  simple-blockalloc patch again.

Note that this version of the ext4-mballoc-prefetch.patch was reverted and replaced by https://review.whamcloud.com/37633

parents.subject: LU-13180 lustre: reserve bit for RDMA-only memory RPC

commit.subject: LU-12988 ldiskfs: mballoc to prefetch groups
commit.message:
LU-12988 ldiskfs: mballoc to prefetch groups

ahead of scanning. prefething is done in 8 * flex_bg groups, so
it should be 8 read-ahead reads for a single allocating thread.
at the end of allocation the allocating thread waits for read-ahead
completion and initializes buddy information so that read-aheads
are not lost in case of memory pressure.
at cr=0 the number of prefetching IOs is limited per allocation
context to prevent a situation when mballoc loads thousands of
bitmaps looking for a perfect group and ignoring groups with
good chunks.

Signed-off-by: Alex Zhuravlev <bzzz@whamcloud.com>
